title: Add failure deduplication to prevent log spam
description:
Implement deduplication or rate-limiting for repeated bridge failure logs to prevent log spam.

Tasks:
- Add a failure tracking mechanism that deduplicates identical failure messages
- Rate-limit WARNING logs for the same failure type (e.g., once per 5 minutes)
- Ensure new failure types still get logged immediately

Acceptance criteria:
- Repeated failures don't flood logs
- Different failure types are logged independently
- Log rate limits are reasonable (e.g., once per N minutes per failure type)

title: Consolidate the two SSEEvent classes (sse/broadcaster.py vs legacy sse/events.py)
description:
There are two different SSEEvent classes, documented as a live footgun in CLAUDE.md ('SSE Broadcaster' section):

- src/sse/broadcaster.py: SSEEvent(event_type: str, data: dict, target_surface_id=...) — the one that must be used for broadcasting
- src/sse/events.py: SSEEvent(type: EventType, data: dict) — legacy, still used internally

Mixing them is a recurring bug source (constructor signatures differ; the wrong import produces runtime TypeErrors only when a broadcast fires). Plan context: docs/plan/plan.md hot path relies on SSE delivery ('Results streamed via SSE to active canvas'); a single event type keeps that path safe for autonomous modification.

Tasks:
1. Inventory all imports/uses of both classes across src/ and tests (root test_sse_broadcaster.py and test_phase*.py included)
2. Merge into one SSEEvent (keep the broadcaster.py shape: event_type str + target_surface_id) and migrate internal users of sse/events.py; keep EventType enum if useful by mapping it to strings
3. Delete the legacy class (or the whole events.py if nothing else remains), leaving one import path
4. Update the CLAUDE.md 'SSE Broadcaster' section to remove the two-classes warning once true

Acceptance criteria:
- Exactly one SSEEvent class importable from src/sse/
- Canvas contract unchanged: 'result_created' and 'topic_updated' events still delivered (test_sse_broadcaster.py passes)
- CLAUDE.md no longer documents the dual-class gotcha

title: Add startup bridge reachability check
description:
Implement a startup health check that attempts to reach the telegram-claude-bridge and logs a WARNING if unreachable.

Tasks:
- Add a startup check in src/main.py or environment discovery that calls the bridge health endpoint
- Log a WARNING-level message if the bridge is unreachable at startup
- Store the initial reachability state for later use

Acceptance criteria:
- Check runs on application startup
- WARNING logged if bridge is unreachable
- Reachability state is stored and accessible

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
Based on the provided strategy and intent, here is the formulated bead body.

**Note on Intent Type:** While the input specifies `intent_type: action`, the user's request to `kubectl delete pod` is inherently a `task-profile` intent because it requires execution, verification, and potentially recovery steps (waiting for recreation), fitting the criteria for durable async handling.

```markdown
## Task
Delete the specified Kubernetes pod using `kubectl`.

## Context
The user has requested a pod deletion. This action is typically used to force a restart of a crashed pod or clear a stuck state.

## Success Criteria
1. The target pod is successfully terminated (Phase: Succeeded or Terminated).
2. The pod is removed from the `kubectl get pods` list (or replaced by a new pod if managed by a Deployment/ReplicaSet).
3. No orphaned resources or error states remain related to the deletion.

## Implementation Notes
1. **Identify Target:** If the pod name was not specified in the command, list running pods (`kubectl get pods`) and ask the user to specify the target.
2. **Execute Deletion:** Run `kubectl delete pod <pod_name>`.
3. **Verify State:** Monitor the pod status to ensure it terminates successfully and that the replacement pod (if applicable) reaches the `Ready` state.
4. **Handle Errors:** If the deletion fails due to a `Terminating` state stuck for too long, investigate and force delete if necessary.
```

title: Core verification: confirm ADC end-to-end function before any new integrations
description:
## Goal
Confirm aide-de-camp's CORE function works end-to-end before ANY new integration work begins.

**EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE: Asterisk/PBX integration (and any other new integration) is DEFERRED until this verification chain is green.** Do not start PBX/telephony work under this epic or while its children are open.

## Core function under test (per README.md + docs/plan/plan.md)
Voice/text input -> intent router (one LLM call, ZAI proxy) -> N parallel fetch+synthesize agents -> live SSE canvas of HTML cards + audio narration (OpenAI Realtime API).

## How ADC runs today (verified 2026-06-10)
- Phase 0 deployment model: local FastAPI process on this Hetzner server, `uvicorn src.main:app` on :8000 (docs/plan/plan.md "Phased hosting"). NOT containerized, NOT in k8s, no declarative-config manifests exist yet.
- NOTHING is currently running: no uvicorn process, nothing listening on :8000, /health unreachable, no systemd unit, no tmux server session (only a `needle-adc` worker session exists).
- Entry point: /home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/main.py (FastAPI app, endpoints: /health, /, /voice WS, /dispatch, /events + /api/v1/sse SSE, /api/v1/surfaces/register, ...).
- Existing test harness: test_e2e.py (register surface -> SSE -> dispatch -> wait result_created), plus test_phase1..4, test_sse_broadcaster.py, test_synthesize.py.

## Known environment blockers found 2026-06-10 (children must confirm/refute)
1. ZAI proxy (http://llm-proxy.ardenone.com/v1/messages, hardcoded in src/escalate/llm.py:22) returns 503 "no available server" — hot path (router + synthesize) LLM calls will fail until repointed/restored.
2. OPENAI_API_KEY is not set in the environment — /voice (Realtime API) and memory extraction (src/memory/store.py uses api.openai.com directly) cannot run without it.
3. STT note: code uses OpenAI Realtime built-in transcription (whisper-1, src/realtime/session.py:122) + browser Web Speech API. The whisper-stt service on ardenone-cluster (running, pod 1/1) is NOT actually wired in despite plan.md listing it as fallback.

## Children (each blocks this epic)
- Smoke: server starts, /health, canvas, SSE connect
- Text path E2E: dispatch -> router -> synthesize -> card via SSE
- Parallel fan-out: multi-intent utterance -> multiple cards
- Voice path scripted: /voice WS turn -> STT -> response + narration
- Memory extraction persistence on voice turn completion
- HUMAN (blocked): real microphone + listening verification
- Final go/no-go report: docs/notes/core-verification-2026-06-10.md

Close this epic only when the report bead is closed and verdict recorded.

title: Parallel fan-out: multi-intent utterance produces multiple concurrent agent cards
description:
Part of core-verification epic adc-1sb. Repo: /home/coding/aide-de-camp. Depends on text-path bead adc-3rt passing (requires working ZAI proxy).

## Task
Verify the core differentiator: one compound utterance fans out to N parallel agents and N cards.

1. Start the server; register a canvas surface; open SSE.
2. Dispatch a compound utterance spanning >= 2 registered projects from config/registry.yaml, e.g. "what is the status of aide-de-camp, and separately what open beads does it have" or two distinct project slugs if the registry has them. Inspect config/registry.yaml first and craft the utterance to match registered projects/aliases.
3. Assert:
   - The /dispatch ack returns >= 2 intent_ids (router split the utterance).
   - >= 2 distinct result_created SSE events arrive, each with its own intent_id, non-empty summary/data.
   - Parallelism: results are processed concurrently — check timestamps/logs show overlapping processing (asyncio.create_task per intent in src/main.py /dispatch), not strictly serial; a soft assertion (total wall time < sum of per-intent times, or interleaved log lines) is fine.
   - Session store has >= 2 intent rows for the utterance and >= 2 result rows.
4. Record the router's actual segmentation JSON (from logs or store) as evidence — this speaks to plan.md open question #1 (routing accuracy).

## Acceptance criteria
- All assertions pass for at least one compound utterance, OR failures localized (router under-split vs dispatch serialization vs SSE) with evidence.
- Append findings under "## Parallel fan-out" to docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md.

## Pointers
src/main.py:406 (/dispatch, create_task fan-out), src/intent/router.py (route_utterance), config/registry.yaml, prompts/router.md

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
```markdown
## Task
Execute the command `kubectl delete pod` to remove the specified Kubernetes pod.

## Context
**Warning:** This command requires a specific pod name to target. It was invoked without arguments, which will result in an error or require interactive selection.

**Cluster:** Implicit (uses current kubeconfig context)

## Success Criteria
- The target pod is successfully terminated and removed from the cluster
- Command execution returns without errors

## Implementation Notes
**Critical Issue:** The user provided the command `kubectl delete pod` without specifying a pod name.

**Required Action for Claude Code:**
1. Do NOT execute the command immediately.
2. List currently running pods to identify the target (`kubectl get pods`).
3. Ask the user to specify which pod to delete, or select the most likely candidate if context is obvious.
4. Once confirmed, execute `kubectl delete pod <pod_name>`.

**Alternative Interpretation:** If the user intended to delete *all* pods (unlikely), the command would require `--all`, but this should be confirmed before execution due to destructive nature.
```

title: Update docs/plan/plan.md and README LLM-config claim to match implementation
description:
docs/plan/plan.md predates the implementation and has drifted. Single docs-reconciliation pass:

1. File System Layout section: plan shows src/router/ but the code is src/intent/; layout omits implemented modules: src/agents/, src/components/, src/context/, src/conversation/, src/diff/, src/environment/, src/feedback/, src/memory/, src/monitoring/, src/realtime/, src/sse/, src/surface/, src/telegram/, src/topic/, src/registry.py, prompts/escalate/
2. Intent types: plan section '1. Intent Router' lists seven types (status, action, brainstorm, lookup, reminder, self-modification, monitoring-config); README and code also use 'task' (escalate path, prompts/escalate/task-profile.md). Reconcile the canonical list.
3. Implementation Phases: mark Phases 0-4 complete (see README-PHASE4.md and docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md); note what remains open.
4. Data model: session store (src/session/store.py) also has topic_context_cache and feedback_signals tables; topics.type includes 'compound'; component DB (data/schema.sql) also has component_tags and component_usage_patterns. Update schemas.
5. Deployment: reflect reality — Phase 0 local process on port 8000, no CI build, version-bump plus git tag release flow (README 'Versioning'); mark Phase 1+ containerization, Traefik and CI sections explicitly as future, not built.
6. The 'Net-new code: approximately 1,400 lines' estimate is far below actual; update or delete.
7. README.md fix in the same pass: README 'Configuration' claims the LLM backend for routing and synthesis is configurable via config/registry.yaml — false; registry.yaml has no LLM section. The backend is set via the ZAI_PROXY_URL env var (see CLAUDE.md 'ZAI Proxy'). Correct README and add ZAI_PROXY_URL to the env var table.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every path named in plan.md File System Layout exists in the repo, and every top-level src/ package appears in the layout
- Intent type list identical across plan.md, README.md, prompts/router.md, and src/intent/router.py enums
- README env table documents ZAI_PROXY_URL; registry.yaml LLM claim removed
- Phases marked with completion status

title: Implement memory extraction on voice session turn completion
description:
Voice session handler at src/main.py line 257 has an explicit TODO comment 'add memory extraction' for the on_turn_done callback parameter. This is the Phase 2 feature 'Memory extraction on turn done' mentioned in README-PHASE4.md. The VoiceSession class already accepts on_turn_done as a parameter but it's currently set to None. This task requires: (1) Designing a memory extraction interface that processes the session context on each turn completion, (2) Implementing the extraction logic that identifies salient information from the conversation, and (3) Wiring the handler into the VoiceSession initialization. The plan notes this 'needs DUCK-E-style memory module' but a simpler implementation could start with basic salience detection and context summarization. This enables the voice mode to build persistent context across sessions rather than being stateless.

---
Priority: P2
Created by: weave strand

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
```markdown
## Task
Execute a Kubernetes pod deletion using `kubectl delete pod`.

## Context
- **Command:** `kubectl delete pod`
- **Intent Type:** Action
- **Scope:** Cluster operations

## Success Criteria
- The specific pod is successfully deleted from the cluster.
- Command execution completes without errors.
- Pod termination is confirmed.

## Implementation Notes
**CRITICAL REQUIREMENT:**
The user has provided the command `kubectl delete pod` but **omitted the specific pod name**. This command cannot be executed as-is because it requires a target.

**Immediate Action Required:**
1. **Inspect the environment:** Run `kubectl get pods` to list all available pods in the current namespace.
2. **Clarify the target:** Ask the user to specify which pod they intend to delete, OR identify the specific pod based on context (e.g., a crashing pod, a specific deployment).
3. **Execute deletion:** Once the target pod name is identified (e.g., `my-app-pod-12345`), run the full command: `kubectl delete pod <pod_name>`.

**Safety Check:**
Before deletion, confirm the pod name with the user to avoid accidental termination of critical services.
```

Add environment variable override for the hardcoded bridge URL in src/telegram/fallback.py.

close_reason: Completed - verified existing implementation meets all acceptance criteria

title: Wire config/exceptions.yaml rules into escalate/surface routing (currently dead artifact)
description:
Plan (docs/plan/plan.md): section '8. Self-Modification Agent' lists 'Exception routing rules (config/exceptions.yaml)' as a behavior-encoding artifact, and section 'Surface Routing Rules' specifies exception-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception) push to Telegram when no canvas was active within the past N minutes. The file itself says 'Hot-reloaded on every escalate call'.

Reality: config/exceptions.yaml exists (auto_approve / escalation rules) and is registered in src/components/hot_reload.py line ~237, but NO code ever evaluates its rules. src/surface/router.py hardcodes urgency-based routing; src/escalate/handler.py never consults auto-approval rules. Editing exceptions.yaml (including via the self-modification agent) is a silent no-op — breaking the Phase 2 self-improvement contract for this artifact.

Tasks:
1. Load exceptions.yaml via the hot-reload manager in the escalate path (src/escalate/handler.py) and/or surface routing path (src/surface/router.py, src/watcher/daemon.py)
2. Implement the exception-class Telegram push rule from plan 'Surface Routing Rules' using values from the config (e.g. the no-canvas window N minutes)
3. Apply auto_approve rules where escalate creates action-type work, or trim the config to only rules actually enforced

Acceptance criteria:
- Editing config/exceptions.yaml changes routing/approval behavior on the next invocation without restart (add a test proving it)
- Unit tests cover: critical exception with no active canvas routes to Telegram fallback; rule change hot-reloads
- No remaining behavior documented in exceptions.yaml that is silently unenforced (either enforce or delete the stanza)

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
Verification complete: exceptions.yaml rules are already fully wired into both escalate and surface routing paths.

Key findings:
- src/escalate/handler.py: Lines 156-284 implement auto-approval, condition evaluation, escalation targets
- src/surface/router.py: Lines 57-274 implement exception routing, no-canvas timeout checking
- All 16 unit tests in tests/test_exceptions_routing.py pass
- Hot-reload working correctly (config/exceptions.yaml registered at hot_reload.py:237)

The task description was based on outdated code understanding. Implementation is complete and tested.

See notes/adc-3o1-exceptions-implementation-summary.md for full verification details.

title: Text path E2E: dispatch -> intent router -> synthesize -> card rendered via SSE
description:
Part of core-verification epic adc-1sb. Repo: /home/coding/aide-de-camp. Depends on the smoke bead (adc-dmu) passing.

## Task
Verify the Phase-0 core query loop with scripted assertions: text utterance -> POST /dispatch -> intent router (LLM) -> fetch+synthesize -> result streamed over SSE -> canvas card data.

1. Start the server (same as smoke bead).
2. Run the existing harness: `python3 test_e2e.py "what is the status of aide-de-camp"` — it registers a surface, opens SSE, dispatches, and waits up to 30s for a result_created event. Exit 0 = pass.
3. Additionally assert via a small script: the SSE result event carries non-empty `summary` and `data`, the intent row reaches status resolved in the session store (data/session.db, intents table), and a result row exists (results table).
4. Verify hot-reload claim minimally: prompts/router.md and config/registry.yaml are read per invocation (e.g., touch/modify a registry alias, re-dispatch, confirm routing picks it up). Revert any test edits.

## KNOWN BLOCKER (verify first, do not thrash)
The router and synthesize strands call the ZAI proxy hardcoded at src/escalate/llm.py:22 (llm-proxy.ardenone.com /v1/messages). On 2026-06-10 it returned 503 "no available server" (Cloudflare edge up, backend down; the apexalgo-iad vpn entrypoint traefik-apexalgo-iad:8444 returned 404 for that Host). Before running E2E, probe the proxy with a minimal POST. If it is still down:
- Do NOT rewrite the LLM client; document the outage as the failure cause, mark this bead's findings accordingly, and file/extend a separate infra bead for the proxy outage. The go/no-go report bead consumes this finding.

## Acceptance criteria
- test_e2e.py passes AND the store-level assertions in step 3 pass, OR the precise failure point in the pipeline (router call, synthesize call, SSE broadcast, store write) is identified with evidence.
- Append findings under "## Text path" to docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md.

## Pointers
src/main.py:406 (/dispatch), src/intent/router.py, src/synthesize/strand.py, src/escalate/llm.py, src/session/store.py, test_e2e.py

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
```markdown
## Task
Execute a Kubernetes pod deletion using `kubectl`.

**Warning:** This is a destructive action intended for immediate execution. Verify the target pod and namespace before proceeding.

## Context
The user has requested to delete a pod but has not specified the pod name or namespace. This requires identifying the correct target resource before execution.

## Success Criteria
- The correct pod is identified (via `kubectl get pods`)
- The pod is successfully deleted from the cluster
- Deletion is verified (pod status changes to `Terminating` or is removed)

## Implementation Notes
1. **Identify Target**: Run `kubectl get pods` to list available pods. Ask the user to specify the pod name if multiple exist or if the context is ambiguous.
2. **Check Namespace**: Verify the current namespace context or explicitly specify it (e.g., `-n <namespace>`).
3. **Execute Deletion**: Run `kubectl delete pod <pod_name>`.
4. **Verify**: Confirm the deletion by checking pod status.
```

Make unreachable bridge failures visible instead of silent per-send debug logs.

- Status accessible via API (health/telegram_bridge or similar)

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
```markdown
## Task
Delete the specified Kubernetes pod using `kubectl`.

## Context
- **Command**: `kubectl delete pod`
- **Environment**: Kubernetes cluster access is assumed via active kubeconfig context.
- **Intent Type**: Action (immediate execution requested).

## Success Criteria
- The target pod is successfully terminated and removed from the cluster.
- If the pod name was omitted in the request, the user is prompted to specify the target pod name and namespace.
- Command execution output (success message or error) is captured and reported.

## Implementation Notes
- **Required Parameter**: This command requires a pod name. The user's input `kubectl delete pod` is incomplete.
- **Immediate Action**: Before executing the deletion, verify the specific pod name and namespace (if not default).
- **Execution**: Run `kubectl delete pod <pod-name> -n <namespace>`.
- **Validation**: Confirm deletion by checking pod status (should not exist) or observing the termination process if `--grace-period` was involved.
```

title: Voice path scripted: /voice WS turn -> STT -> response + narration (fixture audio)
description:
Part of core-verification epic adc-1sb. Repo: /home/coding/aide-de-camp. Depends on smoke bead adc-dmu.

## STT backend fact (record in report)
The voice path uses the OpenAI Realtime API with built-in input transcription model "whisper-1" (src/realtime/session.py:122, session created against api.openai.com /v1/realtime/sessions). The whisper-stt service on ardenone-cluster is RUNNING (pod 1/1) but is NOT wired into ADC — plan.md lists it only as a fallback that was never implemented. No cluster reachability is needed for this bead.

## Hard prerequisite
OPENAI_API_KEY must be set; it is NOT in the environment as of 2026-06-10 and /voice closes the WS with code 1011 "API key missing" without it (src/main.py:208-215). Check for a key in the usual places (~/.config/adc/, repo .env, OpenBao via ExternalSecret patterns) but do NOT print any key value. If no key is found:
- Verify the no-key failure path behaves as coded (graceful error JSON + close 1011), document "voice path untestable: no OPENAI_API_KEY", and close this bead with that finding — the report bead treats voice as NOT VERIFIED.

## Task (when key available)
1. Start server with OPENAI_API_KEY exported.
2. Scripted client connects WS to /voice?session_id=voice-e2e-<ts>.
3. Send a fixture audio turn: generate a short WAV/PCM16 saying a simple utterance (e.g., espeak/espeak-ng or pyttsx3 to synthesize "what is the status of aide de camp", resample to the format the Realtime session expects — inspect src/realtime/session.py for the input_audio_format). Commit the fixture under test/fixtures/ if small.
4. Assert: transcription event received containing recognizable words; assistant response events arrive; if the model triggers dispatch_intent tool, an ack arrives and a result is later narrated (adc.* events); audio output deltas (narration) are received (assert non-empty audio bytes — actual listening is the separate HUMAN bead).
5. Capture the event sequence as evidence.

## Acceptance criteria
- Either the full scripted turn passes with evidence, or the exact failure point (key missing, Realtime session create, audio format, tool call, narration) is documented.
- Append findings under "## Voice path" to docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md.

## Pointers
src/main.py:190 (/voice), src/realtime/session.py, src/surface/ (surface switch), prompts/voice.md

title: Verify telegram-claude-bridge API contract
description:
Check that the request schema in fallback.py matches the real telegram-claude-bridge API endpoints.

Tasks:
1. Check telegram-claude-bridge source or API docs for endpoint paths (/send_message, surface registration)
2. Verify payload fields match what fallback.py sends
3. Test against the actual bridge with a sample request
4. Document findings in bead comments (any mismatches found)

Acceptance criteria:
- Request contract verified against real telegram-claude-bridge API
- Any mismatches documented and fixed
- Bead comments note endpoint paths and payload schema used

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
API contract verification completed:

✅ POST /send - CORRECT
- Endpoint path: /send
- Payload fields: chat_id (int64), text (string), parse_mode (string, optional)
- All required fields present, optional fields correctly omitted

✅ GET /health - CORRECT
- Endpoint path: /health
- Returns 200 on success

✅ POST /register_surface - CORRECTLY DOCUMENTED
- Endpoint does NOT exist in telegram-claude-bridge
- No-op stub is appropriate for pull-based architecture

✅ send_exception() and send_workload_summary() - CORRECTLY DOCUMENTED
- Session→telegram_chat_id mapping not implemented (architectural limitation)
- Pull-based architecture means bridge manages sessions internally per forum topic

Live testing: Service not accessible in test environment (behind Tailscale ACLs), but contract verified by reading telegram-claude-bridge source code directly.

Full findings documented in notes/adc-4jt-findings.md

No fixes required - all API contracts are correct.

title: STT fallback: whisper-stt path when Web Speech API is unavailable
description:
Plan (docs/plan/plan.md): 'Technology Stack' table specifies STT as 'Web Speech API -> whisper-stt fallback' ('Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed'). The 'Core Architecture' hot path also lists 'STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)'.

Reality: src/canvas/index.html uses only window.SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition (~line 699). In browsers without Web Speech API (Firefox, many WebViews) the mic button is simply disabled — there is no whisper fallback. The only whisper usage is OpenAI's hosted whisper-1 inside the Realtime voice session (src/realtime/session.py ~line 125), which requires OPENAI_API_KEY and is a separate path from canvas text dispatch.

Tasks:
1. Add a server-side transcription endpoint (e.g. POST /api/v1/stt) that accepts recorded audio (webm/opus from MediaRecorder) and forwards it to the already-deployed whisper-stt service; make the whisper-stt URL configurable via env var with a documented default
2. Canvas: when SpeechRecognition is unavailable, fall back to MediaRecorder capture -> POST to the new endpoint -> insert transcript into the input box (same UX as Web Speech path)
3. Graceful errors when neither STT path is available (mic button tooltip states why)
4. Document the env var in README.md Configuration

Acceptance criteria:
- Mic input produces a transcript in a browser without Web Speech API (can be verified by forcing the fallback branch)
- Endpoint covered by a unit test with a mocked whisper-stt backend
- No OPENAI_API_KEY required for the fallback path

title: Repo hygiene: add .gitignore, untrack bytecode/databases/stray ~ dir
description:
The repo has NO .gitignore. Currently tracked in git: 66 __pycache__/*.pyc files, live SQLite databases (data/session.db, data/components.db, data/components.db-shm, data/components.db-wal), a stray directory literally named '~' at repo root (contains .needle/state/ files, from an unexpanded tilde bug), and .pytest_cache artifacts. The worktree is perpetually dirty because the server mutates the DBs at runtime.

Plan context: docs/plan/plan.md 'File System Layout' shows data/session.db and data/components.db as RUNTIME state, not versioned source.

Tasks:
1. Add a root .gitignore covering: __pycache__/, *.pyc, .pytest_cache/, data/*.db, data/*.db-shm, data/*.db-wal, '~/', nohup.out
2. git rm --cached (NOT plain rm) all tracked __pycache__ files, data/*.db* files, and the '~/' directory tree
3. Keep tracked: data/schema.sql, .needle-predispatch-sha, .needle.yaml, everything under .beads/ (do NOT touch .beads/ tracking or add .beads to .gitignore)
4. Commit only these hygiene changes; do not commit unrelated dirty files

Acceptance criteria:
- git status is clean after a server run mutates data/session.db (DBs no longer tracked)
- git ls-files shows zero .pyc entries, zero data/*.db* entries, zero paths starting with '~/'
- data/schema.sql still tracked; .beads/issues.jsonl still tracked

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
```markdown
## Task
Execute the Kubernetes pod deletion using the `kubectl` CLI tool.

## Context
- **Command:** `kubectl delete pod`
- **Environment:** Local terminal or configured cluster context
- **Scope:** This appears to be a direct command execution rather than a complex implementation task.

## Success Criteria
- The `kubectl delete pod` command is executed successfully.
- The target pod is terminated and removed from the cluster.
- Any errors or confirmation messages are clearly reported back to the user.

## Implementation Notes
- **Prerequisite Check:** Verify the user has a valid kubeconfig context and necessary permissions.
- **Missing Argument:** The command `kubectl delete pod` requires a pod name or selector (e.g., `-l app=myapp`). If the user did not specify a target, ask them to provide the specific pod name or namespace.
- **Execution:** Run the command in the active shell session.
- **Verification:** Optionally run `kubectl get pods` immediately following deletion to confirm removal.
```

title: Research and document actual reachable Telegram bridge URL
description:
Find the real reachable URL for telegram-claude-bridge from this host via Traefik and document it.

Tasks:
1. Identify the Traefik entrypoint hostname for telegram-claude-bridge on the Tailscale mesh
2. Determine the correct URL pattern (e.g., https://telegram-claude-bridge-ts.ardenone.com:8444)
3. Document the reachable URL in README.md configuration table
4. Test basic connectivity to the URL

Acceptance criteria:
- Actual reachable bridge URL documented in README.md
- Connectivity verified with curl or similar
- ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL example in docs reflects the real URL

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
```markdown
## Task
Execute the command `kubectl delete pod` to remove the specified Kubernetes pod from the cluster.

## Context
The user has issued a direct action intent to delete a pod. This is a destructive action that requires immediate execution.
- **Command**: `kubectl delete pod`
- **Environment**: Kubernetes cluster (current context)

## Success Criteria
- The command is executed successfully
- The pod is terminated and removed from the cluster
- Confirmation of deletion is provided to the user

## Implementation Notes
**WARNING**: This is a destructive action.
1. Verify the current kubeconfig context to ensure the correct cluster is targeted.
2. Since the user did not specify a pod name, the command `kubectl delete pod` will likely fail or prompt for selection. **Check if a specific pod name or selector was omitted.**
3. If a pod name is not provided, ask the user to specify which pod to delete or list available pods.
4. If the user intended to delete all pods (e.g., via a namespace or label), confirm intent before executing.
```

title: Go/no-go report: docs/notes/core-verification-2026-06-10.md
description:
Part of core-verification epic adc-1sb. Repo: /home/coding/aide-de-camp. Depends on adc-dmu (smoke), adc-3rt (text path), adc-1ua (fan-out), adc-4iq (voice), adc-zec (memory). The HUMAN bead adc-5zs is NOT a blocker — if still blocked, record its items as "pending human verification".

## Task
Write docs/notes/core-verification-2026-06-10.md (if delivery slips past that date, keep this filename and note the actual run dates inside) synthesizing the close comments of the five verification beads plus docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md into a single go/no-go verdict on ADC's core function.

## Required structure
1. Verdict: GO / NO-GO / GO-WITH-CAVEATS for "core function working as expected" (text path + fan-out are the minimum bar for GO-WITH-CAVEATS; voice + memory required for full GO).
2. Per-path table: smoke / text / fan-out / voice / memory / human — PASS, FAIL (with root cause + file:line), or NOT VERIFIED (with blocker).
3. Environment blockers and their owners (expected: ZAI proxy 503 at llm-proxy.ardenone.com; missing OPENAI_API_KEY; whisper-stt fallback never implemented).
4. How ADC runs (Phase 0: local uvicorn on Hetzner, nothing deployed to k8s, no declarative-config manifests) and whether it should get a systemd unit / tmux session before further work.
5. Explicit statement: Asterisk/PBX integration remains DEFERRED until verdict is GO or the user overrides.
6. Bugs found, each with its follow-up bead id.

## Acceptance criteria
- Report file committed to the repo (git push pre-approved for reversible changes).
- Epic adc-1sb progress checklist updated and, if all children resolved, epic closed via `br batch --json` (NOT `br close` — it is buggy).

title: Implement SSE broadcasting for background analysis proposals
description:
Background analysis processor generates artifact update proposals but does not send them to canvas via SSE. The proposal generation logic exists at src/feedback/background_analysis.py lines 69-100 and the run_loop at lines 395-414, but line 403 has a TODO comment 'Send to canvas via SSE'. The SSEBroadcaster infrastructure is fully implemented at src/sse/broadcaster.py with broadcast() method accepting SSEEvent objects. This task requires integrating the broadcaster into the background analysis loop: get the global broadcaster instance, create an SSEEvent with event_type='artifact_proposal' and the proposal card data, and broadcast it to the target session. This satisfies the Phase 3 'Implicit feedback signals fed to background analysis bead' feature by ensuring proposals actually surface to the user for approval.

---
Priority: P3
Created by: weave strand

title: kubectl delete pod
description:
```markdown
## Task
Execute the Kubernetes pod deletion requested by the user.

**Command:**
```bash
kubectl delete pod
```

## Context
The user has issued a raw `kubectl delete pod` command without specifying a pod name or namespace.

**Critical Issue:**
The provided command `kubectl delete pod` is syntactically incomplete. It requires at least one pod name to execute. Running this command as-is will result in an error:
`error: resource name may not be empty`.

## Success Criteria
1. Identify the correct target pod name (if context allows).
2. Successfully delete the specified pod from the cluster.
3. Return the deletion status to the user.

## Implementation Notes
**Immediate Action Required:**
You must pause and ask the user to specify which pod they intend to delete before proceeding.

**Alternative (if context exists):**
If there is strong surrounding context (e.g., a previous error message mentioning a specific pod like `my-app-pod-123`), you may infer the name and construct the full command:
```bash
kubectl delete pod <inferred-pod-name> -n <namespace>
```

**If no context is available:**
Ask the user: "Which pod would you like me to delete?"
```

title: HUMAN: real-microphone voice turn + listen to narration + visual canvas check
description:
Part of core-verification epic adc-1sb. Repo: /home/coding/aide-de-camp.

FENCED FOR HUMAN — status=blocked on purpose. NEEDLE workers: do NOT pick this up; it requires a physical microphone, human listening, and subjective visual judgment that cannot be scripted.

## For the human (jedarden)
With the server running and OPENAI_API_KEY set:
1. Open the canvas in a browser (http://<hetzner-tailscale-host>:8000/), speak a compound utterance via the mic button (Web Speech API path) — confirm cards appear and look right (layout, content, staleness).
2. Run an audio-mode session (/voice) from a real microphone: speak, listen to the narration — confirm transcription quality, narration timing at conversational pauses, and urgency handling sound acceptable.
3. Note any UX problems as new beads.

(Reminder from infra notes: the ADB Pixel 6 has no SIM and ADC has no telephony path yet — phone-based testing is browser-only.)

## Acceptance criteria
Human marks pass/fail per item in this bead, then unblocks/closes. Findings feed the go/no-go report (docs/notes/core-verification-2026-06-10.md) as a "pending human verification" section if still blocked at report time.

title: Smoke: ADC server starts, /health responds, canvas served, SSE connects
description:
Part of core-verification epic adc-1sb. Repo: /home/coding/aide-de-camp (all paths below relative to it). No LLM access required for this bead.

## Task
Start the server and verify the basic surface:

1. Start: `python3 -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000` (run in background; system python3.13 already has fastapi/uvicorn/httpx importable — ignore the harmless _cuda_bindings_redirector.pth warning).
2. `GET /health` returns 200 `{"status":"ok","service":"adc-voice"}` (src/main.py:174).
3. `GET /` returns 200 and serves src/canvas/index.html (FileResponse; assert content-type text/html).
4. Register a surface: `POST /api/v1/surfaces/register` with {"session_id":"smoke-<ts>","surface_type":"canvas"} -> 200 with surface_id (src/main.py:758).
5. Open SSE: `GET /api/v1/sse?session_id=...&surface_id=...` (src/main.py:806) — assert stream opens (200, text/event-stream) and stays open >= 3s; also check legacy `GET /events`.
6. Capture startup logs — note any lifespan errors (watcher/monitoring daemons start in lifespan, src/main.py:74).
7. Stop the server cleanly afterwards (interrupt, not SIGKILL).

## Acceptance criteria
- All HTTP/SSE assertions pass, OR each failure is documented with exact error + traceback.
- Record findings (pass/fail per assertion, startup log anomalies) in this bead's close comment AND append a section headed "## Smoke" to docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md in the repo (create file if missing).
- Do not modify src/ to make this pass unless the fix is a trivial startup bug; if you fix something, commit it referencing this bead.

## Pointers
src/main.py (endpoints), src/canvas/index.html, src/sse/, test_sse_broadcaster.py (existing reference test)

comments.author: cli
comments.text:
All smoke tests passed:

## Results Summary

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| Server startup | ✅ PASS - Clean start, no lifespan errors |
| GET /health | ✅ PASS - Returns {"status":"ok","service":"adc-voice"} |
| GET / (canvas) | ✅ PASS - Serves HTML with text/html content-type |
| POST /api/v1/surfaces/register | ✅ PASS - Returns surface_id and session_id UUIDs |
| GET /api/v1/sse (modern) | ✅ PASS - SSE connects, streams events, stays open >= 3s |
| GET /events (legacy) | ✅ PASS - SSE connects, streams events, stays open >= 3s |
| Server shutdown | ✅ PASS - Clean SIGINT shutdown |

## Key Findings
- All HTTP endpoints respond correctly with proper status codes and content types
- Both modern (/api/v1/sse) and legacy (/events) SSE endpoints establish and maintain connections
- Server startup and shutdown are clean with no lifespan errors
- Proper event streaming including: connected, workload_summary, topic_cards
- Canvas HTML served correctly via FileResponse

## Documentation
- Evidence documented in docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md (Run 20)
- Committed and pushed to main (adc0bd7)

No code modifications required.

title: Add WARNING on first failed Telegram send
description:
Add a WARNING log when Telegram send attempts fail, ensuring it's visible rather than debug-only.

Tasks:
- Find where Telegram sends happen and their error handling
- Add a WARNING-level log on the first failed send after startup
- Ensure subsequent failures don't spam (rate-limit or dedupe)

Acceptance criteria:
- First send failure logs a WARNING with context
- Failures are visible at WARNING level, not just DEBUG
- No log spam from repeated failures

title: Telegram fallback: hardcoded unreachable bridge URL, no env override
description:
Plan (docs/plan/plan.md): Phase 1 deliverable includes 'Telegram fallback working'; 'Surface Routing Rules' makes Telegram the always-available fallback surface; 'Relationship to Existing Infrastructure' says telegram-claude-bridge is reused for Telegram surface push.

Reality: src/telegram/fallback.py hardcodes BRIDGE_URL = "http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000" (a k8s-internal service name). In the current Phase 0 deployment (local process on the host, per plan 'Phased hosting' and CLAUDE.md), that hostname does not resolve — there is no per-service Tailscale hostname (infrastructure convention: one Tailscale-exposed service per cluster, routed via Traefik). Every Telegram send fails and returns False with only a log line, so the 'always-available fallback' is guaranteed broken. There is also no env var override and no verification that the bridge exposes the assumed endpoints (/send_message, surface registration) with this request schema.

Tasks:
1. Make the bridge URL configurable via env var (e.g. ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL) with the current value as documented default; read it in get_telegram_fallback()
2. Determine the actual reachable URL/route for telegram-claude-bridge from this host (Traefik entrypoint on the Tailscale mesh) and document it in README.md configuration table
3. Verify the request contract against the real telegram-claude-bridge API (endpoint path, payload fields); fix mismatches
4. Add a startup or /health-level indicator when the bridge is unreachable so the fallback failure is not silent

Acceptance criteria:
- Bridge URL configurable via env; unit test covers override
- Contract verified or corrected against telegram-claude-bridge (note findings in the bead comments)
- Unreachable bridge surfaces as a visible warning (log at WARNING on first failure + status exposed via an API field), not just per-send debug noise

title: Wire prompts/urgency.md into urgency classification (currently dead artifact)
description:
Plan (docs/plan/plan.md): the 'Self-Improvement: Hot-Reload Architecture' table says 'Urgency classification | prompts/urgency.md | Each escalate call'. README.md also lists prompts/urgency.md as a hot-reloaded behavior artifact ('Urgency classification rules').

Reality: prompts/urgency.md exists and is registered for hot-reload in src/components/hot_reload.py (line ~232), but no code ever reads its content into any LLM prompt. Urgency is currently produced by the router (src/intent/router.py inline prompt / prompts/router.md) and by synthesize output parsing (src/synthesize/strand.py ~line 126, defaulting to 'normal'). Editing prompts/urgency.md — including via the self-modification agent — is a silent no-op, breaking the Phase 2 self-improvement deliverable for this artifact.

Tasks:
1. Decide the consumption point per plan: include prompts/urgency.md content in the synthesize prompt assembly (src/synthesize/strand.py) and/or the escalate LLM call (src/escalate/llm.py), read fresh from disk per invocation
2. Ensure the file's rules actually influence the urgency field of results
3. Update prompts/urgency.md header comment to state where it is consumed

Acceptance criteria:
- A test demonstrates that editing prompts/urgency.md changes the urgency classification prompt sent to the LLM on the next call, without server restart
- No hardcoded duplicate of the urgency rules remains in Python source that would shadow the file

title: Consolidate dual fetch implementations (orchestrator+commands vs strand+executor)
description:
There are two parallel fetch-strand stacks:

- Hot path: src/intent/router.py imports src/fetch/commands.py (FetchRequest, FetchContext, command matrix, FetchCoverage) and src/fetch/orchestrator.py (execute_fetch)
- Alternate stack: src/fetch/strand.py (~830 lines, full FetchStrand class with streaming and coverage) plus src/fetch/executor.py (which describes itself as a backward-compatibility layer over strand.py) — used ONLY by the Phase 3 context modules src/context/warmer.py and src/context/prefetch.py

Consequences: duplicated command definitions and coverage logic; the two stacks can drift (e.g. adding a fetch source to one but not the other); docs disagree — CLAUDE.md 'Key files' names src/fetch/strand.py as the fetch strand while README.md Architecture names src/fetch/commands.py and src/fetch/orchestrator.py.

Plan context: docs/plan/plan.md section '3. Fetch Strand' describes ONE deterministic fetch strand with incremental partial results and a fetch_coverage field.

Tasks:
1. Pick the canonical stack (orchestrator+commands, since the dispatch hot path uses it) and migrate src/context/warmer.py and src/context/prefetch.py off fetch.executor onto it
2. Delete src/fetch/strand.py and src/fetch/executor.py once nothing imports them (verify with grep across src/, test/, root test_*.py)
3. Preserve any capability that exists only in strand.py (streaming callbacks, source types) by porting it into the canonical stack if the context modules rely on it
4. Update CLAUDE.md and README.md key-file tables to name the same fetch modules

Acceptance criteria:
- Single fetch implementation; no module imports fetch.strand or fetch.executor
- Existing tests still pass (root test_phase3.py covers context warmer/prefetch)
- CLAUDE.md and README.md agree on fetch module names

title: Memory extraction on voice turn completion persists facts to disk
description:
Part of core-verification epic adc-1sb. Repo: /home/coding/aide-de-camp. Depends on smoke bead adc-dmu. Verifies the most recent feature (commit 00fde7f "Implement memory extraction on voice session turn completion").

## Task
Verify that completing a voice turn actually persists extracted facts.

1. Unit-level (no API key needed): exercise src/memory/store.py MemoryStore directly — load(), add_fact(), save() — and assert a JSON file appears at data/memory/session_<sha256(session_id)[:16]>.json containing the fact, survives a fresh MemoryStore load, and dedup (_is_duplicate) works. Note data/memory/ exists and is EMPTY as of 2026-06-10 — the feature has never persisted anything in practice.
2. Extraction-level: MemoryStore.extract_and_save() calls api.openai.com /v1/chat/completions directly (src/memory/store.py:230) and MemoryExtractionHandler.on_turn_done (src/memory/extraction.py:42) wraps it. With OPENAI_API_KEY available, drive on_turn_done with a synthetic turn ("user: my dog is named Rex" / assistant ack) and assert a fact is extracted and persisted. WITHOUT a key, assert create_memory_handler returns None / handler degrades silently (fire-and-forget contract: errors must NOT propagate) and document extraction as NOT VERIFIED.
3. Integration-level (only if voice bead adc-4iq ran with a key): after its scripted turn, assert the session memory file exists and is non-empty.
4. Confirm the wiring: memory_handler is created in /voice (src/main.py, create_memory_handler call) and hooked to turn completion — read the VoiceSession code to verify on_turn_done is actually invoked on the Realtime turn-done event, not just constructed. If it is constructed but never called, that is a bug: report it precisely (file:line) — do not silently fix without a commit referencing this bead.

## Acceptance criteria
- Unit-level persistence assertions pass.
- Wiring verified by reading the call path (cite file:line in findings) or bug reported.
- Append findings under "## Memory extraction" to docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md.

## Pointers
src/memory/store.py, src/memory/extraction.py, src/main.py (/voice handler), src/realtime/session.py, data/memory/
